plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name limit.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure as per conservative mandate.
Trim QQQI to 15% to reduce the flagged position while maintaining income exposure.
Increase QQQ to 25% to boost broad index exposure for stability.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into the Healthcare sector and reduce tech concentration.
Allocate 15% to cash to create a significant capital preservation buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces overall risk by cutting leveraged ETFs and increasing cash, but will likely have lower growth potential and smaller drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely and trim SPAL to 2% to reduce overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 10% to manage the flagged position and reallocate capital.
Increase SMH to 15% to lean into a strong sector with a good scanner read.
Buy GOOGL at 8% to add a high-quality tech name from the scanner list.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer to provide flexibility and manage risk.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while introducing quality tech, balancing growth potential with moderate drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration limit.
Increase SOXL to 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductor exposure, while selling SPAL.
Trim QQQI to 5% to reallocate capital from the flagged income position.
Increase SMH to 20% to concentrate in the strong semiconductor sector.
Buy CRDO at 10% as a high-scoring scanner setup to maximize growth potential.
Maintain a 5% cash buffer to keep capital highly invested for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It significantly increases exposure to leveraged ETFs and concentrated tech, leading to higher potential returns but also much larger drawdowns and the risk of leverage decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital for income assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure for stability.
Increase QQQI to 30% to significantly boost income-focused exposure.
Trim QQQ to 10% and sell CRWD to reduce growth-oriented and high-beta exposure.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and provide stability.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. It significantly de-risks by cutting leveraged and high-beta assets, but growth potential is minimal. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
